title: Devices and methods for fluid infusion, drainage and collection
abstract: Containers for collecting fluid are provided that include a reservoir comprising a plurality of panels enclosing an interior, side panels of the reservoir are movable from an expanded position towards to a compressed position and biased to the expanded position for generating a vacuum within the interior. An elongate housing extends along a top side of the reservoir including a passage extending between proximal and distal ends thereof and communicating with the interior of the reservoir. An elongate member extends through the housing including a distal extension, a proximal extension, and a lumen extending therebetween. One-way valves are coupled to the distal and proximal extensions for permitting fluid flow proximally from the distal extension into the interior of the reservoir via and permitting fluid flow proximally from the proximal extension while preventing fluid flow distally out the distal extension and distally into the proximal extension and lumen.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A container for collecting fluid, comprising: a reservoir comprising a plurality of panels enclosing an interior thereof, the panels comprising side panels generally opposite one another that are movable from an expanded position towards one another to a compressed position and biased to the expanded position for generating a vacuum within the interior; an elongate housing extending along a top side of the reservoir and comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a passage between the proximal and distal ends communicating with the interior of the reservoir; an elongate member extending through the housing between the proximal and distal ends, the elongate member comprising a distal extension extending from the housing distal end, a proximal extension extending from the housing proximal end, and a lumen extending between the distal and proximal extensions, the lumen comprising an opening communicating with the passage; a distal one-way valve coupled to the distal extension for permitting fluid flow proximally from the distal extension into the interior of the reservoir via the lumen and opening while preventing fluid flow distally out the distal extension; and a proximal one-way valve coupled to the proximal extension for permitting fluid flow proximally from the proximal extension while preventing fluid flow distally into the proximal extension and lumen. 2. The container of claim 1, wherein the passage extends longitudinally along the housing at least partially between the proximal and distal ends, and wherein the housing further comprises a window extending between the proximal and distal ends to permit viewing fluid within the passage. 3. The container of claim 2, further comprising indicia on or adjacent the window to indicate a volume of fluid within the interior of the reservoir and passage when the housing is oriented substantially vertically. 4. The container of claim 2, wherein the plurality of panels of the reservoir are configured to conceal fluid within the interior of the reservoir. 5. The container of claim 4, wherein the plurality of panels of the reservoir are opaque. 6. The container of claim 1, further comprising an elongate recess in the housing extending partially from the proximal end towards the distal end, the recess configured to receive the proximal tubular extension when the proximal tubular extension is folded and inserted into the recess, thereby preventing fluid flow through the proximal tubular extension. 7. The container of claim 1, wherein the plurality of panels further comprising a bottom panel generally opposite the top side of the reservoir and end panels coupled to the side panels and the bottom panel to enclose the interior. 8. The container of claim 1, wherein the elongate member extends through the housing adjacent the passage, the opening comprising an opening in a side wall of an intermediate portion of the elongate member. 9. The container of claim 1, further comprising a visual indicator on the housing or the reservoir configured to provide a visual output indicating pressure status of the interior of the reservoir. 10. The container of claim 9, wherein the visual indicator comprises one or more LEDs configured to emit a color confirming whether the pressure within the interior exceeds a desired pressure threshold. 11. The container of claim 1, wherein the elongate member is disposed within the housing. 12. The container of claim 1, wherein the elongate member is substantially permanently fixed relative to the housing such that a fixed length of the elongate member defines the proximal and distal extensions. 13. The container of claim 1, wherein the side panels are biased towards the expanded position. 14. The container of claim 1, wherein the side panels pivot relative to one another when directed between the expanded and compressed positions. 15. The container of claim 14, wherein the side panels define planes that intersect one another in the expanded position. 16. A container for collecting fluid, comprising: a reservoir comprising a plurality of panels enclosing an interior thereof, the panels comprising side panels generally opposite one another that are movable from an expanded position towards one another to a compressed position and biased to the expanded position for generating a vacuum within the interior; an elongate housing comprising a valve device extending along a top side of the reservoir and comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a passage between the proximal and distal ends communicating with the interior of the reservoir, the valve device comprising an elongate member having a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen therethrough extending between the proximal and distal ends of the valve device, and at least two ports communicating with the elongate member; wherein the elongate member communicates with a distal one-way valve and the passage; wherein the distal one-way valve is coupled to the elongate member permitting fluid flow proximally from the valve device while preventing fluid flow distally; a proximal extension having a proximal and distal end with a lumen therethrough coupled to the proximal end of the elongate housing communicating with the passage; and a proximal one-way valve coupled to the proximal extension for permitting fluid flow proximally from the proximal extension while preventing fluid flow distally into the proximal extension and lumen. 17. The container of claim 16, wherein the valve device comprises a first port that may be selectively opened for infusion of fluids and a second port configured to couple to a catheter. 18. The container of claim 16, wherein the elongate member through the valve device comprises a distal extension coupled to the distal end of the elongate housing. 19. The container of claim 16, further comprising an actuator for selectively isolating the second port from the first port. 20. The container of claim 16, further comprising a source of fluid coupled to the first port to deliver fluid from the source via the first port into the proximal extension when the actuator isolates the second port from the first port. 21. The container of claim 16, further comprising an actuator for selectively isolating the distal one-way valve, the reservoir, and the proximal extension from the first port. 22. The container of claim 21, further comprising a source of fluid coupled to the first port to deliver fluid from the source via the first port into the second port when the actuator isolates the proximal extension from the first port. 23. The container of claim 16, further comprising a safety lever engageable to prevent the side panels from being subsequently directed towards the compressed position to maintain the vacuum within the interior. 24. A container for collecting fluid, comprising: an elongate housing comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a first opening in a side wall of the housing extending partially between the proximal and distal ends and a second opening in the housing generally opposite the first opening and extending partially between the proximal and distal ends, thereby defining a passage extending between the first and second openings; a window covering the first opening to permit viewing fluid within the passage; a reservoir coupled to the housing and including a plurality of panels enclosing an interior of the reservoir communicating with the second opening, the plurality of panels comprising side panels generally opposite one another that are movable from an expanded 

 position towards one another to a compressed position for generating a vacuum within the interior and evacuating fluid from the interior; and a tubular extension extending from each of the proximal and distal ends of the housing comprising a one-way valve configured to prevent fluid flow from the reservoir out the distal end and into the reservoir from the proximal end. 25. The container of claim 24, further comprising indicia on or adjacent the window to indicate a volume of fluid within the interior of the reservoir and passage when the housing is oriented substantially vertically. 26. The container of claim 24, wherein the plurality of panels of the reservoir are opaque. 27. The container of claim 24, wherein the side panels of the reservoir are biased towards the expanded position. 28. The container of claim 24, further comprising an elongate recess in the housing extending partially from the proximal end towards the distal end, the recess configured to receive the tubular extension extending from the proximal end of the housing when the tubular extension is folded and inserted into the recess, thereby preventing fluid flow through the proximal tubular extension. 29. The container of claim 28, wherein the recess comprises a first region having a cross-section smaller than the tubular extension such that the tubular extension is compressed when inserted into the recess. 30. The container of claim 29, wherein the recess comprises a second region larger than the first region configured to receive the one-way valve when the tubular extension is inserted into the recess. 31. The container of claim 24, further comprising a visual indicator on the housing or the reservoir configured to provide a visual output indicating pressure status of the interior of the reservoir. 32. The container of claim 31, wherein the visual indicator comprises one or more LEDs configured to emit a color confirming whether the pressure within the interior exceeds a desired pressure threshold. 33. The container of claim 24, wherein the side panels pivot relative to one another when directed between the expanded and compressed positions. 34. A method for collecting fluid within a container, comprising: providing a container comprising a reservoir defining an interior and compressible to from an expanded position to a compressed position to reduce a volume of the interior, the container further comprising an elongate member coupled to a housing coupled to the reservoir, the elongate member comprising a fluid path from a distal extension through the housing to a proximal extension that communicates with the interior of the reservoir; coupling a drainage device to the distal extension; compressing the reservoir to the compressed position, a one-way valve in the distal extension preventing air within the interior from flowing back through the distal extension into the drainage device such that the air is evacuated out the proximal extension; releasing the reservoir such that reservoir is biased to return towards the expanded position, thereby generating a vacuum within the interior to draw fluid from the drainage device through the distal extension into the interior; and securing the proximal extension within a recess in the housing to prevent leakage from the interior via the proximal extension. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein securing the proximal extension comprising folding the proximal extension back towards the distal extension and inserting the proximal extension into the recess such that a one-way valve on the proximal extension is received in a pocket adjacent the recess. 36. The method of claim 34, further comprising orienting the housing such that a window on the housing is oriented substantially vertically, whereby fluid within the interior flows through a passage in the housing adjacent the window to align with indicia on or adjacent the window to identify the volume of the fluid within the reservoir. 37. The method of claim 34, further comprising, after fluid has been collected within the interior: removing the proximal extension from the recess; and compressing the reservoir to evacuate fluid from the interior out the proximal extension, the one-way valve in the distal extension preventing the fluid from flowing back through the distal extension into the drainage device. 38. The method of claim 34, wherein the reservoir is configured to conceal fluid within the interior of the reservoir. 39. The method of claim 34, wherein, after releasing the reservoir, a visual indicator on the housing or the reservoir is activating to provide a visual indication that the pressure within the interior exceeds a predetermined pressure threshold. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the visual indicator provides a visual indication when the pressure within the interior falls below the predetermined threshold. 41. The method of claim 34, further comprising activating a valve device coupled between the drainage device and the distal extension to provide access into the flow path from a port. 42. The method of claim 41, further comprising: isolating the drainage device from the port; and infusing fluid into the port such that the fluid flows through the distal extension into at least one of the interior and the proximal extension. 43. The method of claim 41, further comprising: isolating the drainage device from the distal extension; and infusing fluid into the port such that the fluid flows into the drainage device and not through the distal extension. 44. A method for collecting fluid within a container, comprising: 

 providing a container comprising a reservoir defining an interior and compressible to from an expanded position to a compressed position to reduce a volume of the interior, the container further comprising an elongate member coupled to a housing coupled to the reservoir, the elongate member comprising a fluid path from a distal extension through the housing to a proximal extension that communicates with the interior of the reservoir; coupling a drainage device to the distal extension; compressing the reservoir to the compressed position, a one-way valve in the distal extension preventing air within the interior from flowing back through the distal extension into the drainage device such that the air is evacuated out the proximal extension; releasing the reservoir such that reservoir is biased to return towards the expanded position, thereby generating a vacuum within the interior to draw fluid from the drainage device through the distal extension into the interior; and orienting the housing such that a window on the housing is oriented substantially vertically, whereby fluid within the interior flows through a passage in the housing adjacent the window to align with indicia on or adjacent the window to identify the volume of the fluid within the reservoir. 45. The method of claim 44, further comprising engaging a safety mechanism to prevent the reservoir from being directed towards the compressed position while collecting fluid. 46. The method of claim 45, further comprising disengaging the safety mechanism and compressing the reservoir towards the compressed position to evacuate fluid from the reservoir or generate a vacuum.
